disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No fever, rigors, or shaking chills
trigger_question_examples: Have you felt hot and cold or had rigors?
Is your temperature up at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever or chills, I've felt my forehead and I seem normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No jaundice, dark urine, pale stools, confusion, or easy bruising/bleeding
trigger_question_examples: Any yellowing of your eyes or skin, dark urine or pale stools?
Have you been unusually confused, drowsy, or bruising/bleeding easily?
Do your eyes look yellow in the mirror?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my eyes and skin look normal, urine and stools are normal, no confusion or bruising.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is mild, vague, reproduced by pressing, and started right after the alarming SMS
trigger_question_examples: When exactly did the ache start in relation to the text message?
Does pressing on the area bring on the ache?
How severe is the pain out of ten?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started right after the text, it's mild, and yes pressing on it seems to bring it on.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No recent paracetamol overuse, new medications, or herbal/recreational hepatotoxins
trigger_question_examples: Have you taken a lot of paracetamol or any new tablets/supplements recently?
Any herbal remedies, recreational drugs, or extra painkillers?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't taken any painkillers or new supplements.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: I read that liver failure can kill you overnight — do I need to rush to emergency right now?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Address the catastrophic health belief calmly and accurately: screen for the features that WOULD indicate an emergency (fever/jaundice/confusion), and if absent, explain that a mildly abnormal chronic liver result does not cause acute liver failure and does not need an emergency tonight, while arranging prompt GP review.
